scope_note: QA SL(2,Z) Versor Isomorphism cert [296]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.X (SL(2,Z), continued fractions, Euclidean algorithm); Hestenes, D. and Sobczyk, G. (1984) Clifford Algebra to Geometric Calculus, Reidel, ISBN 978-90-277-1673-6 (versor algebra, Pin/Spin groups, sandwich product); Brocot, A. (1861) Calcul des rouages par approximation, Revue Chronometrique 3:186-194 (Stern-Brocot tree). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): (C1) Stern-Brocot bijection: every primitive (b,e) with gcd(b,e)=1 maps to a unique word W in {L,R}* via W*[1,1]^T=[b,e]^T (left-to-right matrix product, left-accumulated; all W in SL(2,Z), det=+1). (C2) T-operator identity: the QA T-step (b,e)->(e,b+e) equals M*[b,e]^T where M=[[0,1],[1,1]], det(M)=-1 (M in GL(2,Z) not SL(2,Z): odd-grade versor as operator). (C3) Rotor identity: M^2=L*R exactly as integer matrices; two T-steps = one SL(2,Z) rotor (det=+1). (C4) Singularity fixpoint: (9,9) is the unique fixed state of T in {1,...,9}^2 under QA A1 arithmetic (((9+9-1)%9)+1=9). (C5) Orbit partition: T acting on {1,...,9}^2 yields exactly one orbit of size 1 (Singularity), one of size 8 (Satellite), three of size 24 (Cosmos) = 1+8+72=81 total. Note: grade of OPERATOR M^k alternates (det(M^k)=(-1)^k) but SB words for STATES always have det=+1 (states are in SL(2,Z)+). Mechanism: Theorem NT maintained (all matrix entries integer; no continuous input). Builds on cert [294] (SL(2,Z) Spine) and cert [291] (Fibonacci orbit periods, 1/8/72 partition). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
